cache_policy.disease_page_update_policy: Use source URLs for public references and cached copies only for local reproducibility when downloads are approved.
cache_policy.full_source_commit_policy: Do not commit full source pages unless redistribution is explicitly permitted and recorded.

maturity_target.goal: Move the cerebral infarction disease chapter toward a 100-page source-review maturity target before expert-review status.
maturity_target.summary: The source pack now identifies well over 100 pages of guideline, textbook-style, radiology teaching, and review material, including AHA/ASA, ACR, NICE, ESO, NCBI Bookshelf, and open-access imaging literature. The chapter still needs deeper extraction and clinical expert review before it should be treated as mature.

purpose: Track starter source URLs and reuse notes for creating cerebral-infarction.md.

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for stroke-related imaging appropriateness, including arterial stroke scenarios, completed ischemic stroke, hemorrhage, vascular imaging, and evidence-method context.
sources.notes: Use for modality selection and imaging workflow context, not for patient-specific treatment decisions.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.title: ACR Appropriateness Criteria Cerebrovascular Diseases-Stroke and Stroke-Related Conditions

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Current professional society guideline support for acute ischemic stroke care, including imaging-driven selection contexts, thrombolysis/thrombectomy updates, pediatric AIS considerations, and early complication management.
sources.notes: Use for current guideline context and to supersede older 2018/2019 guidance where there are conflicts. Keep treatment details at research-context level.
sources.page_count_estimate: large guideline; use as a major maturity source
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.title: 2026 Guideline for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke
warnings: Use official AHA/ASA and Stroke DOI pages for final citation details before mature publication.

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Guideline context for acute stroke and transient ischemic attack diagnosis and early management in adults, including urgency, imaging, and system-of-care framing.
sources.notes: Use as an additional professional guideline perspective, especially for diagnosis/initial-management context and missing-information prompts.
sources.page_count_estimate: guideline length; contributes to 100-page maturity target
sources.title: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s: diagnosis and initial management

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad clinical reference support for ischemic stroke etiology, TOAST-like categories, lacunar and vascular-territory patterns, pathophysiology, differential diagnosis, prognosis, and complications.
sources.license: CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 for the Bookshelf chapter; do not modify or embed locally unless attribution and reuse constraints are acceptable.
sources.notes: Use to strengthen natural history, etiology, syndrome patterns, prognosis, differential diagnosis, and outcome context.
sources.title: Ischemic Stroke - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf
warnings: Reuse is noncommercial/no-derivatives; keep figures link-only unless that restriction is acceptable for the target distribution.

sources.claim_breadth_supported: European guideline support for thrombolysis, DWI-FLAIR mismatch, CT/MRI core-perfusion mismatch, ADC threshold context, and late/unknown-onset imaging selection strategies.
sources.notes: Use for guideline-backed imaging-selection and treatment-context sections, especially DWI-FLAIR mismatch and perfusion mismatch language.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.title: European Stroke Organisation guidelines on intravenous thrombolysis for acute ischaemic stroke

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Open-access evidence for acute ischemic lesion appearance on DWI, lesion-shape variability, artifact confounding, manual annotations, and automated lesion segmentation context.
sources.content_type: PMC Open Access package metadata plus selected JPG figures
sources.notes: Use for local reusable figures and AI/segmentation context. This is a methods paper, not a clinical guideline.
sources.title: Fully automatic acute ischemic lesion segmentation in DWI using convolutional neural networks
warnings: Use as supportive visual and segmentation-method evidence, not as a broad clinical authority.

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for acute ischemic stroke guideline context, including emergency imaging, DWI-FLAIR mismatch, vascular imaging, and treatment-window implications.
sources.notes: Historical guideline-summary context retained for traceability. Prefer the 2026 AHA/ASA source for current guidance when there are conflicts. Avoid turning this chapter into treatment guidance.
sources.title: 2019 Update to the 2018 Guidelines for the Early Management of Acute Ischemic Stroke

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad textbook-style overview of stroke imaging modalities, CT signs, CTA/CTP, MRI, DWI/ADC, and clinical significance.
sources.notes: Use as broad imaging review source. Recheck last-update date before mature publication.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.title: Stroke Imaging - StatPearls - NCBI Bookshelf

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad radiology teaching support for early CT signs, MRI/DWI/ADC appearance, perfusion mismatch, and acute stroke workflow.
sources.notes: Use as clinician-facing radiology teaching support. Publication is older; anchor guideline claims to ACR/AHA.
supported_sections: Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.title: The Radiology Assistant: Imaging in Acute Stroke

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad review support for MRI sensitivity, multimodal imaging, thrombolysis selection context, and prognosis estimation.
sources.license: Open access article; verify reuse details before local embedding.
sources.notes: Use as review support, with full text review needed before mature publication.
sources.title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging in Acute Ischemic Stroke

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow technical support for ADC, DWI, T2, and FLAIR signal evolution and lesion-age interpretation.
sources.notes: Use only for timing/evolution claims; do not treat as a complete clinical guideline.
supported_sections: Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.title: Evolution of Apparent Diffusion Coefficient, Diffusion-weighted, and T2-weighted Signal Intensity of Acute Stroke
warnings: PMC full text may require browser challenge in this environment; use PubMed/PMC metadata and review full text later.

sources.claim_breadth_supported: Teaching/reference support for DWI/ADC acute ischemic stroke appearance and timing caveats.
sources.notes: Use as teaching reference with citations/revision history, not as sole support for broad guideline claims.
sources.title: Diffusion-weighted imaging in acute ischemic stroke
2026 Guideline for the Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke: A Guideline From the AHA/ASA
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad current guideline support for acute ischemic stroke management from prehospital evaluation through early complications and early secondary prevention.
sources.notes: PubMed citation and DOI anchor for the 2026 AHA/ASA guideline already represented by the AHA professional page. Use for final citation details.

sources.title: Imaging of Ischemic Stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad support for ischemic core imaging with noncontrast CT, DWI, ADC, FLAIR, vascular signs, perfusion/collateral assessment, and DWI lesion-pattern etiology clues.
sources.notes: High-value broad review with multiple figure candidates for DWI patterns, serial DWI/ADC/FLAIR evolution, and mismatch imaging.
warnings: Figure reuse requires review; keep link-only until permissions are checked.

sources.title: Imaging of Ischemic Stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad review support for acute stroke MRI protocol, T2/FLAIR timing, DWI/ADC cytotoxic edema, perfusion imaging, and MRA/CTA context.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Useful for sequence-by-sequence MRI protocol and CT/MR perfusion examples; older but still a detailed imaging teaching source.
warnings: Older review; anchor current treatment-pathway claims to current guidelines.

sources.title: Neuroimaging Modalities Used for Ischemic Stroke Diagnosis and Monitoring
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad review of CT, CTA, CTP, MRI DWI/ADC/FLAIR/SWI, ultrasound, PET/SPECT, monitoring, and AI applications in ischemic stroke.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.notes: Use to expand multimodal and monitoring context, including DWI/ADC evolution and T2 shine-through caveats.

sources.title: Imaging in Acute Stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad emergency imaging review for noncontrast CT, CT perfusion, CTA, DWI, and acute stroke workflow.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Use as supporting emergency-imaging review; older but helpful for CT and DWI basics.
warnings: Older review; current workflow claims should be checked against AHA/ACR.

sources.title: Comparison of CT and MR imaging in ischemic stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Scope-specific support for CT versus MRI strengths, perfusion imaging, DWI core, SWI hemorrhage detection, and posterior fossa/small lesion sensitivity.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Useful for CT/MRI modality-choice contrasts and figure candidates.

sources.title: What's new in imaging of acute stroke?
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad-to-scope support for modern acute stroke imaging workup, NCT/CTA/PCT, MRI DWI/FLAIR/GRE/SWI, large-vessel occlusion, and mismatch imaging.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Good source for workflow diagrams and imaging protocol evolution.

sources.title: Imaging of acute stroke prior to treatment: current practice and evolving techniques
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad-to-scope support for acute CT signs, CTA/CTP, MRI minimum protocol, DWI, T2*, FLAIR, mimics, and treatment-selection imaging.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Useful for acute CT signs and practical pre-treatment imaging; older but detailed.
warnings: Use current guideline sources for modern eligibility claims.

sources.title: Stroke and Stroke Mimics: Diagnosis and Treatment - Diseases of the Brain, Head and Neck, Spine 2020-2023
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad textbook-style support for stroke and stroke-mimic imaging, acute ischemic stroke imaging selection, LVO/EVT context, and mimic differential.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Good authoritative source for mimic-aware review and imaging selection context.
warnings: Check chapter license before embedding figures locally.

sources.title: MR-DWI in the acute stroke diagnosis
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Scope-specific current educational support for DWI, ADC, DWI/FLAIR mismatch, DWI/PWI mismatch, pseudonormalization, T2 shine-through, and false positives/negatives.
supported_sections: Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.notes: Updated 2026 practical reference. Use as supportive educational material, not primary guideline evidence.

sources.title: Use of MRI (DWI, SWI, FLAIR) in Acute Ischemic Stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Scope-specific radiology teaching support for DWI, SWI, FLAIR, acute infarct core, false negatives, and mimics.
sources.notes: Useful because the transcript includes expert radiology phrasing; verify access and licensing before local use.
warnings: May require subscription for full access.

sources.title: Stroke Imaging - Learn Neuroradiology
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Practical teaching support for stroke presentation, imaging evaluation, CT/CTA/CTP, MRI DWI/ADC/FLAIR, vascular territories, cases, complications, and mimics.
sources.notes: Strong video-teaching source for humans and agents; includes multiple embedded YouTube videos and case examples.
warnings: Use as teaching support; cite guidelines/reviews for formal claims.

sources.title: Stroke and Its Mimics: Diagnosis and Treatment - Diseases of the Brain, Head and Neck, Spine 2024-2027
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for acute stroke imaging protocols, MRI findings, pitfalls, and stroke mimics.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Use as newer textbook-style source for MRI protocols, DWI/ADC/FLAIR appearance, and code-stroke interpretation pitfalls.

sources.title: Non-Contrast MRI Sequences for Ischemic Stroke: A Concise Overview for Clinical Radiologists
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for sequence-specific MRI characterization and stroke phase timing.
supported_sections: Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.notes: Useful for staging early hyperacute through chronic infarct with DWI, ADC, FLAIR, T1/T2, SWI/GRE, ASL, and spectroscopy context.

sources.title: Wake-Up Stroke: Clinical Characteristics, Imaging Findings, and Treatment Option - an Update
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for DWI-FLAIR mismatch and perfusion-diffusion mismatch as imaging-selection concepts in unknown-onset stroke.
supported_sections: Evidence Of Active Disease, Progression, Or Recurrence
sources.notes: Use for lesion-age and tissue-at-risk framing, not for standalone treatment recommendations.

sources.title: Wake-Up Stroke and Stroke of Unknown Onset: A Critical Review
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for DWI-FLAIR mismatch performance, limitations, and interpretation caveats.
sources.notes: Useful for adding caveats around visual mismatch variability, sensitivity, specificity, posterior circulation, and lesion-size effects.

sources.title: Mimics and chameleons in stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for clinical stroke mimics and chameleons, including seizure, migraine, sepsis, syncope, metabolic, functional, and mass-like presentations.
sources.notes: Supports mimic-aware differential prompts and cautions that negative CT does not exclude ischemic stroke.

sources.title: Implementation of emergent MRI for wake-up stroke: a single-center experience
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow for operational MRI workflow and DWI-FLAIR mismatch interpretation in wake-up stroke.
sources.notes: Use as narrow implementation evidence; do not generalize beyond workflow feasibility and interpretation context.

sources.title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) - Stroke Manual
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for MRI sequence interpretation and concise DWI/ADC/FLAIR teaching.
sources.notes: Useful for practical terminology such as true diffusion restriction, T2 shine-through, T2 washout, and T2 blackout.

sources.title: Overview of imaging modalities for an Acute Stroke - Stroke Manual
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for acute stroke modality selection, CT/CTA/CTP/MRI roles, and workflow context.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Use for practical acute imaging workflow language and modality tradeoffs.

sources.title: Emergency Department Evaluation and Management of Patients with Transient Ischemic Attack and Acute Stroke - Canadian Stroke Best Practices
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for urgent brain and vascular imaging workflow in suspected acute stroke.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Canadian guidance; use as workflow context and label jurisdiction-specific recommendations.

sources.title: Acute Ischemic Stroke Treatment - Canadian Stroke Best Practices
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for imaging selection concepts in EVT and acute ischemic stroke treatment pathways.
sources.notes: Use to describe why infarct-core estimates, ASPECTS, CT perfusion, CTA, and MRI matter in research context.

sources.title: Triage and Initial Diagnostic Evaluation of Transient Ischemic Attack and Non-Disabling Stroke - Canadian Stroke Best Practices
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Broad for brain and vascular imaging after acute disabling or non-disabling stroke or TIA.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Supports timing and vascular imaging prompts; jurisdiction-specific.

sources.title: Canadian Stroke Best Practice Recommendations: Vascular cognitive impairment, 7th edition practice guidelines update, 2024
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for chronic infarcts, lacunes, microinfarcts, atrophy, white matter disease, and MRI sequence context.
supported_sections: Serial Imaging Assessment And Interval Change
sources.notes: Use for chronic infarct and vascular cognitive impairment context rather than acute stroke triage.

sources.title: Portable, low-field magnetic resonance imaging enables highly accessible and dynamic bedside evaluation of ischemic stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow for low-field portable MRI detection of ischemic stroke lesions.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use as narrow emerging-technology evidence; not a substitute for standard high-field MRI guidance.

sources.title: Enhanced Detection of Acute Ischemic Stroke With Low-Field MRI
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow for low-field DWI/ADC pulse-sequence and hardware improvements in acute ischemic stroke.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use only for low-field MRI technology context and sensitivity caveats.

sources.title: Brain imaging with portable low-field MRI
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for low-field MRI capabilities and limitations across neurological indications including stroke.
sources.notes: Use for technology context and limitations, not core disease appearance.

sources.title: Diffusion-Weighted Imaging, MR Angiography, and Baseline Data in a Systematic Multicenter Analysis of 3,301 MRI Scans of Ischemic Stroke Patients
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for DWI lesion distribution and MRI cohort phenotyping.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use for research endpoint and lesion-distribution implications, not for individual clinical diagnosis.

sources.title: An MRI Based Ischemic Stroke Classification - A Mechanism Oriented Approach
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for mechanism-oriented DWI lesion pattern classification.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use for lesion-pattern/vascular-territory classification and lacunar-versus-nonlacunar distinctions.

sources.title: Digital 3D Brain MRI Arterial Territories Atlas
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for MRI arterial territory mapping and large-scale lesion analysis.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Useful for agentic lesion-localization and cohort-labeling context.

sources.title: Mapping the Supratentorial Cerebral Arterial Territories Using 1160 Large Artery Infarcts
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for supratentorial arterial territory mapping from DWI infarct cohorts.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use for territorial patterns and atlas-driven cohort design.

sources.title: Magnetic resonance diffusion-perfusion mismatch in acute ischemic stroke: An update
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for diffusion-perfusion mismatch patterns, penumbra, and perfusion caveats.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use for PWI/DWI mismatch and tissue-at-risk concepts with dated-method caveats.

sources.title: Automated DWI-FLAIR mismatch assessment in stroke using DWI only
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow for automated DWI-FLAIR mismatch prediction and AI decision-support context.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use as algorithmic/agentic context; keep claims scoped to study design and validation cohorts.

sources.title: Artificial Intelligence for Automated DWI/FLAIR Mismatch Assessment on Magnetic Resonance Imaging in Stroke: A Systematic Review
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for AI methods addressing DWI/FLAIR mismatch and human-rater comparison.
supported_sections: Statistical Implications Of Treatment And Progression
sources.notes: Use to frame AI-assisted mismatch evaluation and validation limits.

sources.title: Stroke protocol (MRI)
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for MRI stroke protocol sequences and practical interpretation purposes.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Teaching/reference source with citations and revision history; not a professional guideline.

sources.title: Flair MRI in acute stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Narrow for FLAIR and DWI appearance in a left MCA acute stroke case.
sources.notes: Use as illustrative case evidence only.

sources.title: Imaging approaches for acute ischemic stroke
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for high-level imaging approach and late-window selection context.
supported_sections: Other Modalities And When They Matter
sources.notes: Video/source evidence; intended for healthcare professionals.

sources.title: Efficacy and safety of MRI-based thrombolysis in wake-up stroke: project reporting
sources.claim_breadth_supported: Moderate for historical DWI-FLAIR mismatch rationale and WAKE-UP study context.
sources.notes: Use as project/background context; prefer peer-reviewed trial publications for clinical claims.
